title: Guerdon
content:
Upon the white cheek of the Cherub Year
        I saw a tear.
    Alas!    I murmured, that the Year should borrow
        So soon a sorrow.
    Just then the sunlight fell with sudden flame:
        The tear became
    A wondrous diamond sparkling in the light - 
        A beauteous sight.

    Upon my soul there fell such woeful loss,
        I said, "The Cross
    Is grievous for a life as young as mine."
        Just then, like wine,
    God's sunlight shone from His high Heavens down;
        And lo! a crown
    Gleamed in the place of what I thought a burden - 
        My sorrow's guerdon.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ella Wheeler Wilcox, titled "Guerdon", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
